description: A platter of cheeses served with complementary items such as crackers, bread, fruit, nuts, olives, or preserves. The selection often balances soft, semi-hard, hard, and blue cheeses with sweet and savory accompaniments.
preparation_steps: Select a range of cheeses with different textures and flavor strengths.
Bring the cheeses slightly toward room temperature before serving.
Arrange the cheeses with suitable accompaniments on a board or plate.
Provide knives or spreaders for serving.

nutrition.carbohydrates: Low from cheese alone; higher with crackers or fruit

health_advice.benefits: Provides protein and calcium and can be portioned flexibly for sharing.
health_advice.risks: Can become high in saturated fat, sodium, and calories if portions are large.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Balance rich cheeses with fruit and reasonable cracker portions rather than relying heavily on processed extras.

dietary_notes.note: A cheese-only plate is vegetarian for many people, though some cheeses use animal rennet. Gluten-free versions require gluten-free crackers or bread.

allergens: Gluten if served with crackers or bread
